extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won the sprint after a mechanical in the final 3km forced a bike change, overcoming chaos created by ROGLIČ-EVENEPOEL intermediate sprint skirmishes.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ refused cooperation with EVENEPOEL on late-race move, prioritizing intermediate sprint seconds over potential stage-winning breakaway coordination.
SOLER Marc attacked solo on final climb specifically to contest intermediate sprint, forcing Bahrain to chase and neutralize GC rival LANDA.
Jumbo-Visma initiated intermediate sprint leadout with 8.4km remaining, triggering early EVENEPOEL response that burned matches before finale sprint.
GROVES overcame bike change inside 3km by maintaining composure while rivals sprinted early, winning despite mechanical disadvantage.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: executed sprint perfectly after forced bike change in final 3km, demonstrating exceptional technical handling under pressure
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: allowed emotions over ROGLIČ non-cooperation to dictate tactics, wasting energy contesting intermediate sprint with anger rather than calculation
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: refused cooperation with EVENEPOEL despite mutual breakaway interest, prioritizing 1-second intermediate bonus over potential stage gain
rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: solo attack targeting intermediate sprint forced Bahrain to chase, leapfrogging LANDA into 4th GC by one second
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: team burned energy chasing SOLER's intermediate sprint attack, ultimately conceding GC position by failing to contest bonus seconds
rider_signals.SCHELLING Ide: positive: initiated winning sprint train for Bora-Hansgrohe, demonstrating leadout strength despite GROVES' late bike change
rider_signals.VAN GILS Maxim: positive: strong sprint finish for Lotto despite no leadout, confirming fast-finisher status beyond expected capabilities
